title: Phase 2: action endpoints + auth (command-intent pattern)
source: CEO request 2026-05-29: live web dashboard to monitor the service + active strategy; explicitly think ahead to a control plane (expose actions/functions, portfolio mgmt, corporate actions, multi-strategy). Stack approved: FastAPI+uvicorn+Jinja/htmx.
why: Expose actions from the web WITHOUT breaking the single-broker-actor invariant: POST /api/actions/{manual_reset,flatten,pause,resume,set_leverage} validate (pydantic) -> auth -> write signals/live/commands/<ts>.json; the execution service grows an idempotent, audited command consumer on each tick. Auth mandatory before any action, especially on a real account.
scope: command-intent schema + idempotent consumer in the live loop (TDD).
risk-officer review + CEO gate per action type.

history.reason: feat(dashboard): phase-1 read-only live monitor (FastAPI) for the survival book
